MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-10-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jennifer Alesio, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Vincent Montesano

RULING:
DEFENDANT CRANE CO.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Crane Co.'s motion for summary judgment and its alternate motion for summary adjudication (exposure to asbestos) are denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing packing from Crane Co. valves. Mr. Montesano's deposition and written discovery responses and the deposition testimony of Mr. McLean and Mr. Pantaleoni, which were identified and discussed in plaintiff's discovery responses, preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Dr. Bruch's declaration creates a triable issue whether such exposure was a substantial factor contributing to plaintiff's risk of developing an asbestos-related disease. Defendant's objections to Dr. Bruch's declaration are overruled. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)
